response: I would probably be stressed if I was busy, but maybe a good kind of stressed depending on what I'm busy with. I'd probably also feel tired and drained if I was busy.

A social expectation to be busy would make me feel stressed. I would feel like I was being forced into a competition for something I have no passion for. I would end up feeling exausted and unfulfilled.

As someone who feels tired and depressed most days, I personally experience annoyance at the societal expectation for people to always be busy. I believe that most people are TOO busy with work, chores, taking care of kids, etc. that they have no time to just relax and enjoy life.

Because of these expectations, I often feel proud when I stay busy, even if I haven't really accomplished anything. Logically, I feel it should not be this way, but societal expectations have made me feel this way. I hope that it will not always be like this.

Being bussy makes me feels stressed out because I have no time to relax. It makes me feel restless and with no energy. In addition, this social expectation makes me feel pressured. I feel constraint; like I cannot be myself or who I want to be.

Concerning the social expectation to be busy, I feel annoyed and irritated that others impose such expectations on others. At the same time, I feel slightly understanding since sometimes I find myself thinking it's helpful to be busy in some cases. I feel more nonchalant when it comes from close friends rather than if it comes from institutions or acquaintances. Times when this expectation was imposed on me, I felt pressured and stressed at times.

Emotions that I would feel from this social expectation is most likely irritation. I am someone who is pretty productive but I do like to have free time to myself and away from others. If it were a requirement that I follow this expectation though, then I may definitely feel stressed and exhausted. I would not want to be a part of that society.

Feeling busy all of the time would stress me out. I think there is a distinction between being productive and being overworked. If I am constantly expected to go above and beyond then I would get anxiety at the fear that im not doing enough even during times when it is not warranted. The expectation should be to finish whatever job that is required of me but not to be busy just for the sake of staying busy.

Feelings that come to mind with the expectation listed above are stress and pressure. Being a young professional in this society, I feel that if I am not always busy, it creates feelings of laziness. There is a pressure that builds in you and your subconscious that always brings thoughts that you can be doing more. And if you are not doing more, then there is someone out there like you who wants your position or the promotion you want that is working while you have decided to stop and this creates even more stress.

Honestly, I feel exhausted most of the time and can't help but wonder if all this necessary. The more technological advancements in the workplace I see the more stressed and pressured I feel to have more resources and a better job.
I'd mostly feel stress. I'm not very good at hustle culture. I tend to try to avoid increasing stress in my life. I'm not very driven. I don't like cultures that exploit people for their work ethic.
I already know I would feel anxiety and stress with this social expectation to be busy. I like the feeling of being in demand, but at the same time, it would weigh on me mentally.

I am a bit nervous and second guessing what I am typing. I feel kind of happy because usually I am good at multitasking and getting things done pretty quick. I am experienced in moving and working quickly, so I know that I got this, and am sort of confident, but nervous that I will fail.

I am actually constantly busy at work. It makes my boss value me more as an employee and a member to my company, however, it does make me feel stressed and anxious. It's a double edged sword because I feel like I constantly need to be busy to be valued. That isn't the case though. Whenever I'm not busy at work, I worry that people view me as less.

I am confident with the expectation to always feel busy. I like always being busy and the stress that may bother others does not bother me. I am confident in my ability to prioritize and ensure that everything gets done. I believe that being busy makes the day go by faster.

I am totally fine with the expectation to be seen as "busy". I only care what people view me as on a surface level because that is all that most people will see. If I am able to consistently meet and exceed what the expectations of society are then I will be happy then I will be happy because I will be free to do whatever I want in the time that no one sees me. It is a win win situation for me.

I definitely agree that there is immense social expectation and pressure to be busy. I constantly feel as though I need to be doing something productive. If I ever take even a little bit of time for a break or to do something fun, I feel guilty and like I don't deserve a break. I feel like I need to keep working. At the same time, though, constantly working and trying to be busy makes me exhausted and stressed out. But if I'm not busy, I feel stressed because I start to think that I'm not doing enough or I'm being lazy. I find it difficult to find a balance between work and leisure.

I don't appreciate social expectations to be busy.  I feel there should be a clear separation of work and private life.  What I do on my own time is no one else's business and their judgements about it are meaningless.  At work, I will work as much as is need to fulfill the specifications of my job and no more (unless there is some other benefit).

I don't feel anything because I do not care. I mind my own business and bop around doing my own thing all the time. I am probably more busy than anyone I know and I get things done. Indifference should be my middle name.
I don't like feeling busy. When I feel busy, I feel like there's stuff I'm missing, or things I'm forgetting. I like to take things at my own pace. When things feel busy, it also feels rushed.

I feel a lot of anxiety and depression about the expectation to be busy. I have mental health issues that keep me from even working a part-time job in a stable manner, so I feel utterly lazy compared to the rest of the population. I also feel a bit of jealousy that people have the energy to be so busy. I feel like I can't live a full life in our current society because I can't keep up with the quick pace of everything.
I feel fine when I am busy because it means I am distracted.

I feel frustrated about the expectation to feel busy.  It is not a bad thing to have a break.  In fact, breaks can be very beneficial for work.  I am frustrated that our society sees not being busy as being lazy and uninterested.  Being busy all the time just stresses me out.
I feel immensely pressured and stressed. I don't like to feel this way and I naturally feel the urge to tell them to screw off.

I feel it takes advantage of the worker to some extent. Workers are expected to be busy at all times and therefore productive all jobs when in reality this isn't realistic. I currently work such a job and it's exhausting and wears down on you physically and mentally.

I feel like I'm not doing enough with my life. I feel like everyone else is involved in so much and I never really do anything, and a lot of the time I just sit in my room all day. I feel like I never get anything done and that makes me feel like a failure and like everyone else is ahead of me in life and are doing better than me. I feel inadequate and ashamed and feel like I'm disappointing my parents.

I feel like life is about balance. I'm not really concerned about somebody else's social expectations on how I should live my life. Life these last 5 years have felt exactly as described, too fast, everybody moving at the speed of light, and not taking the time to enjoy themselves, the beautiful things around them, and even the little things that makes your day complete. I personally try to take the time more often, and to work hard and be consistent with my work, but also take the time to take a step back and look and enjoy the fruits of my hard work and labor.

I feel like there is always a want for us to be busy and doing things. the whole day calls for our attention. I do not like it. I enjoy time alone and to myself to think and process the day. I love to be alone for hours and have quiet or things exactly like I need them to be for me. Others do not understand.
I feel like we have this defined construct that people feel they always need to be busy. Sometimes downtime is invaluable to worth just as much as hard work.

I feel negatively about the social expectation to be busy. I feel as though my life is my own to do with what I want. I do not feel that I should be obligated to be busy and stressed just to appear productive.

I feel overwhelmed and stressed, but then feel like I need to act like I am not.
I would internally be feeling like I couldn't handle it all but would project that I have it all together to anyone on the outside.
I feel pressured by society's expectations. Always being busy is stressful, and I don't think it's a fair expectation.
I feel pretty weird facing the social expectation of being busy, because i know a lot of people who just say they are busy because its easy. No one really likes people who are always accessable.

I feel proud when I am busy doing my job at work. And one of the major reasons for this feelings is that I take my job very seriously and i always meet to target. Also whenever I am at my job I give my all and i do not feel distracted in my work in any way.
The feelings i have when i am busy include feelings of pride and happiness.

I feel so much pressure to be as good as everyone else when there is a standard such as this set for me. I don't want to have to feel inadequate and I don't want to feel like people are better than me just because they are busier than I am. If I am not as productive as someone else does that make me a bad person? I am going to be negative to myself now because I am not perfect and I don't meet the standards set every single day that I am supposed to. This makes me feel sad and now I am in a slump. If this standard was not set I would not have been in a slump in the first place and would not have felt depressed. Now I have to get myself back up again and make myself feel better,

I feel that given our societies expectations during this time and age can be very stressful. I’ve found my self at times years ago over estimating my abilities at work and trying to “bite off more than I can chew” in order to impress or show people I’ms hard worker. This ultimately dug me in to a hole and was so stressful to pull myself out.

I feel that there is pressure to maintain a state of being busy. It makes me question my worthiness. I feel scared, nervous, and guilty if I am not doing something productive. I worry about the fear of missing out (FOMO). There is an expectation of always being on that makes me worry that I am not doing enough. On the other hand, when I have a few events happening, I feel excited to connect and engage with others. I like having many things to look forward to.

I feel that you should put self-care first because a job will try to burn you out and you should have boundaries. I think you should listen to your body and if your body wants to take a break you should do that. You should not burn yourself out due to fear of being fired. You are a person and your health comes first. I feel empowered to support my wellness.
I find it stressful. I feel like I need to work myself to death to please society. I feel like balance between work and leisure isn't valued enough.

I get stressed out when I am not busy. I would feel competent, challenged, and fulfilled to face the social expectation to be busy. I would feel competent, because I am expected to stay busy, which means others have faith in my competence in what I'm doing. I would feel challenged, because I would want to stay busy as well as make the best effort with my time. I would feel fulfilled, because I like staying busy, and this would keep me happy.

I get stressed out when I think about society expecting me to be busy constantly. I feel like I will never be able to meet their expectations and I will constantly fail. Also, I enjoy down time to relax or catch up from the week.

I have had to face the social expectation to be busy and it was extremely stressful and anxiety-inducing. I was always on edge, irritable, and tired. I never felt like I was actually accomplishing anything, even though I was constantly on the go. It made both my personal and work relationships difficult during that time.

I have lived that expectation before to always be busy, especially when I was a college student. You had to, it seemed, always be involved in 3 clubs along with demanding class and study schedule. It was exhausting. I think thats what made me realize the slower-paced life was for me. I am doing much better now and can balance all of life's demands with more ease now being less busy!

I honestly feel like I'm constantly busy due to the thoughts that race through my head non-stop on a daily basis. It is so stressful to be expected to be busy always. Feeling like you have no down time to focus on you or your emotions brings such an overwhelming sense of guilt when you can't accomplish everything that you feel the need to, whether they're necessary or not.

I might feel overwhelmed if there was a social expectation to be busy if I were not used to being busy as it is. I live a pretty busy, fast-paced lifestyle with juggling work, friends, family, and other activities. I would feel like nothing has really changed if there were a social expectation to be busy.

I often feel pressured by society to be busy. Even if I'm working at home, I feel like my family thinks I'm not working enough if I relax after doing some work. It feels stressful and frustrating to always have expectations put on me. It doesn't feel like I can ever do enough. Everyone is expected to work until they're dead-tired, which makes me sad that we're all wasting so much of our lives just to work. It would feel much better if our society was able to focus on living our lives more, and people could make more money for their productive hours, like people did in the past.

I personally do feel a social expectation to be busy, particularly at work. I am anxious to be put up for a promotion, so I always ensure that I go above and beyond my work expectations and work hard to be busy at all times. This makes me feel stressed often during my day because I do not always have enough time to actually work on all the tasks that I have accepted. I do want to make sure I do a good job, and I feel that I have been overworking myself. I do not feel particularly more tired than usual, but I do feel that the stress of trying to stay busy has made me more mentally fatigued than anything.

I personally feel outraged at the expectation that to be a person of worth that you need to constantly be toiling away in "productivity". I believe this "productiveness" or "business" is actually just a distraction that we are forced into. If you do not abide by the societal expectations (college etc) then you will literally be enslaved by money by only having jobs available to you which are insufficient for doing anything other than that job. You will only be able to afford to keep working at that job to keep existing. If you do follow the societal expectation of going to college, you will spend many years and all of your time learning a skill and accrue massive amounts of debts, also ensuring you become enslaved to making financial ends meet. This is all just to keep you distracted and working, always trying to make financial ends meet, never realizing the true nature of humanity and our place in this universe, spiritually.

I personally thrive on the feeling of being busy, as it makes me feel competent and having worth. BUT I do think the fast paced burnt-out culture is a detriment to everyone's mental and physical health. I will put in as much effort as required, but there is not paycheck that is worth more than my health and wellbeing
I think I would feel extremely drained. There is only so much you can do and but get super tired from. It’s just the awful feeling of “big brother” and

I think I would feel like a rain cloud was over my head. Good mental health requires you to sit and think about nothing, and that does not jive with this concept. And being a hard worker does not necessarily mean filling your schedule entirely.
I think I would feel overwhelmed really. I don't think fast-paced is good and I think people really need to slow down a lot of the time  because face paced means no peace. No time to think

I think it's a tragedy that my culture places such value on always being busy, occupied, or otherwise trying to accomplish some measurable task. I feel at my best, and my most human, when I can take as much time as I want doing what I want, mainly relaxing. People brag about missing sleep and skipping vacations to get more work done. I think this is awful and it makes me feel unwelcome here. I wish I could move to a place that valued rest and relaxation and good conversation for conversation's sake.

I think that the social expectations to be busy are definitely real. It can be a good or a bad thing. I feel that it is good because it motivates people to work hard and get things done. On the contrary it can lead to feeling like you can never do enough and no matter how much you do it's still not good enough.
I think the expectation to be busy is an arbitrary burden and should not be as emphasized at the expectation to be productive.

I think the social expectation to be busy is unnecessary. You can be productive and still have down time. I think society use to value work like balance more in the past. Now, there is an expectation to be constantly busy and stressed, which I think is stupid. It is unnecessarily stressful and doesn't give anyone a chance to be in the moment and enjoy life. I enjoy being "busy" if I am doing something I enjoy. When I am occupied with things I don't like, it is agitating and feels like a waste of time.

I think this social expectation is unnecessary. I wish more people would take a slower pace and not feel the need to stay busy. It is mentally stressful. I feel this social expectation may have been made by employers for their benefit. It makes me feel sad and angry.

I typically feel the social expectation to be busy.  It feels stressful, like pressure.  It makes me anxious and rushed.  We rarely ever get a moment to take in our surroundings and just be.  It makes my mind rush and race from thought-to-thought as I try to cross items off of my to-do list.  It's a chaotic and scrambled feeling.

I understand why there is a social expectation to be busy, but I would say busy and productive, not being busy just to be busy. I think there is some added pressure and stress that comes with it, but sometimes there are things that just need to be done. Of course, it is also fine to take a day off every now and then as well. I think I would rather be kept busy than just sitting at home all day doing nothing because then I'm not consumed by my thoughts and I am distracted

I would be feeling overwhelmed if I had to be busy all the time. I would probably be stressed and angry. I actually do this myself and try to always be busy at work. It does take a toll on me. I feel mentally drained after a week of work.

I would experience a need to busy to feel valuable. I would probably feel guilty when I wasn't busy because I wouldn't feel accepted in society. I would also probably experience physical, mental, and emotional exhaustion as a result of being busy. Business would add stress and pressure to my life.

I would feeel obligated to work without working, just mindless working. That type of work is dangerous because the preception is that I am always doing something, bt in reality, it is just me appearing to be busy. Not only is that an actualy waste of time, it is yjust mentally exhausting to "feel"busy the entire tie a t work. I would rather do an 1 hour of actualy worok then 8 hors of sort o doing somehing. I would be tired of all the time when I'm not doing something, which is a paradox

I would feel a little stressed about the need to constantly be busy. While I am busy most of the time, it is stressful and worrisome to be too busy. I do not like to be too busy so that I do not have time for other things because then I feel like I do not get the most out of life. It also gives me satisfaction to be busy and for people to think I am busy since that seemingly gives my life more purpose so people will think that I might be more successful than I am. It brings me a conflict of emotions to be busy, especially in the face of others.
I would feel a lot of pressure. I think I would feel guilty if I wasn’t busy or deciding to relax. I would feel like I needed to keep moving.
I would feel a mixture of emotions in this situation. I would feel competent and happy but also kind of irritated that they expect me to be busy all the time

I would feel annoyed as I work very competently doing my current work responsibilities very thoroughly. Appearing busy and adding on to my work load would not be the most effective thing to do, as mistakes can happen. If I end up making a mistake I would feel very disappointed in myself.

I would feel annoyed. I think that I'm a super productive worker, but the way I work doesn't "look" busy to the typical person. But it's just how my brain processes things. I don't think I'd feel too stressed, because I don't think it's my own problem. I think it is a problem for society and the bosses around me, and that they're in the wrong.
I would feel constantly active and on the go.  There would be so much to juggle and I would feel stressed at times as well.
I would feel exhausted having to be busy all the time just to prove myself at work or in school.
I would feel irritated and resentful about feeling the need to be busy. I would hate that my mental health is not valued. I would feel devalued. I would not feel happy or any positive emotions.

I would feel like a slave to the feeling of being busy. I'd feel like I wouldn't have enough alone time to myself in order to do the things I like I feel like I wouldn't be able to see my family or friends as much. Furthermore, I feel like I'd be more inclined to pick up extra shifts at work to keep busy.

I would feel like I am constantly being pulled in different directions, and like I can't spend long periods of time doing any one thing because I have to keep going. I think I would often feel irritated, worried, and stressed because there's always something else to be done. I think I would be experiencing the highs and lows of emotions, but often hiding it until I have the time to deal with it. I would often be tired, exhausted from a day where I just had to push through but then my brain would struggle with the inactivity needed to let me sleep.

I would feel looked up at like a role model to follow. It makes me feel like I have to be busy so that others will see me as important. Not living up to this expectation would make me feel like I am a failure and that I have no life. I want to be seen as a person that is always busy because ot makes me feel accomplished.
I would feel okay. I do my work and get it done. I see no reason to complain about this when just about everyone does some work. I just go with the flow. I do not like to cause issues.

I would feel over whelmed and bothered by the social expectation of being busy. Sometimes enjoying life is okay, but is still seen as weird or odd. Even on vacation, you're expected to be "busy." I would also feel this anger combine with my other emotions and ask myself, "How has society gotten to this point?"

I would feel overwhelmed and anxious in this fast paced environment of always being busy. I would feel a sense of accomplishment of getting more stuff done, but I like having time to relax. Always being busy would make me feel constrictive and locked down. I would worry that I wasn't doing enough to stay busy and any down time would make me feel like a failure.

I would feel overwhelmed and burnt out. I can't stay busy all of the time, otherwise I'll crash. That being said, staying business enables me to keep some negative thoughts out of my head which is a good thing. Idle hands can really be the devil's workshop.

I would feel overwhelmed and stressed out if I felt like I always had to be busy and working on something. Honestly, I already feel this way a fare amount of the time, and I'm often trying to fight against that feeling and allow myself to take a break and chill.

I would feel overwhelmed when considering all of the social expectations that are imposed on me to be busy. I would feel anxious and upset knowing that there is a never-ending laundry list of tasks on my plate. I would also feel exhausted from having to balance so many tasks and having to occupy my schedule all the time. I would feel guilty if I couldn't keep up with all of my obligations. I would also feel incompetent if others were busier than I was, which I'm sure is how the situation would play out.
I would feel overwhelmed, yet determined and calm

I would feel overworked and burned out. I would feel as though i need to put on a fake face and be someone who i am not. I would be depressed and feel like i have no time for the things i love and would feel lonely.

I would feel pressured and exhausted having to live up to this expectation, knowing it personally and having experienced it in the past. However I would also feel somewhat competent because being constantly busy is a sign of productivity and doing well in life.
I would feel pretty calm about it since I really do not get stressed about anything in life. If the expectation is to be busy then i will stay busy. Should be easy for me to do

I would feel pretty content. I pride myself on being productive, not necessarily busy, so I am able to adjust well to the pressures of society. I would not overwork myself, but I wouldn’t be a sloth either.

I would feel several emotions at the same time. I would feel frustrated. However, I would feel slightly positively or negatively depending on the tasks. My emotions would depend on the nature of the tasks at hand.

I would feel stressed and overworked if I had to stay busy and look like I'm good enough to survive in the society. It adds extra pressure and sometimes, you can't even think straight because you're trying so hard to fit in. I've been down the trajectory where being busy was seen as a great thing and being overworked and underslept is seen normal. I've broken out of that mindset but now,I feel like I'm not doing enough. There is more productive ways I could be spending my time or be doing more with the available time that I have compared to what I'm doing right now.

I would feel stressed because I would feel like I have to constantly be busy and productive. I would feel like taking time to relax is a waste of time and would feel pressured to always be working instead. Eventually I would feel burnt out and feel even worse about myself for having less of a drive to always be working and productive with my time.

I would feel stressed because of this busy lifestyle. I would feel I could never have down time to relax or do activities I enjoy. I would be anxious due to the expectation that I must deliver quality whenever I work. I would feel jealous of other people who can take it easy and do not have such a heavy workload.
i would feel stressed if i felt i had to be busy because society expected me. My work load would probably suffer because i may feel i have to rush to keep busy

I would feel stressed out because if I didnt follow what was expected I would be judged and valued less than people who are more busy than I am. I feel frustrated sometimes because people associate success with how busy someone is. People who are more busy are expected to be smart and are valued as being worth more. If your a busy person people will feel special when they get to spend time with a busy person because they feel special that they got to spend time with that person.
I would feel stressed out if I was always expected to look busy. This would cause me to tire out as I would always have to be looking like I'm doing something even if there is nothing to do.

I would feel stressed out to meet the social expectation. That stress would make me feel drained and exhausted. If I wasn't able to meet it, I would feel depressed and worthless. If I was able to keep myself busy as society expects me, I would feel proud of my abilities.
I would feel stressed out, but I would also try to make a plan to achieve all of my goals. I would want to achieve everything because society wants me to, as well as for myself.
I would feel tired and stressed. It would be a feeling of having no place in the world.

I would feel very annoyed to have the expectation of always being busy.  I do not like this way of life and think it is unproductive to me.  It is also not enjoyable.  I want to be able to be productive for a reasonable amount of time, then relax and enjoy the rest of my time.  I think being always busy is not enjoyable and not able to do long term because I feel I would get burned out.  I think that we should work on setting a new expectation of reasonable productivity and enjoyment of life.
I would feel very stressed and overwhelmed. I am not used to people needing me or needing to be by my side constantly.

I would feel very stressed if I was extrememly busy. Being busy means I will have to multitasks and it will add a more headache and problems upon myself. It was cause me to be tired and make me feel very sleepy as well too. The emotions would just be stress and tired.

I would hate it. Being busy can be good, but it can also be bad. Sometimes people use being busy to run away from their problems. This oftentimes makes problems worse. This is why I would hate being pressured into being busy by social expectations. It would also make me sad because there are times when I like to just be. Meditation and relaxation can be good for the soul, but are often not compatible with a "busy" lifestyle. It would make me sad if I felt I could no longer do them.

I would honestly just feel stressed. I don't like the work culture expects you to hustle all the time. I believe there's a time to get work done, but I think it's equally important to take time to relax because if you're constantly busy, you will definitely get burnt out. I'd also feel a lot of pressure to succeed and I feel like I'd start relying on the validation of others to get going, which is very unhealthy. I'd be much happier if I didn't care what people thought and just did things at my own pace. I believe people shouldn't dictate your actions and you shouldn't put too much weight on people's expectations.

I would personally feel indifferent about this social expectation. I always believe that people are entitled to live however they want and so I do just that. I would never let this expectation make me feel stressed or tired because that would be counterproductive. I would simply ignore this and live my life as fast paced or slow paced as I want.

I would personally feel overwhelmed and exhausted in the face of social expectations to always be busy. I think it would be absolutely draining to feel like my value as a person is diminished if I do not have a million things to do at all times. I think that the feeling of accomplishment after doing some of the tasks I set out to do would even be diminished. I say that because there would be no time to celebrate or enjoy what I'd accomplished. it would simply be time to move on to the next thing.I don't see many if any positive emotions coming to me in the face of this social expecattion to be busy.

I would personally feel stressed and pressured with this expectation to remain busy. I would feel as though I have an expectation to meet and if I don't meet it, I will disappoint those who are observing. Under this pressure, I would feel as though I didn't have time to relax and be myself. I would be living up to other people's expectations.

I would personally feel stressed and tired most of the time. Although, it would be nice to be needed and wanted, too much of this would just lead to me being miserable and feeling awful. I think it would be too much of a good thing and I wouldn't like it at all. It would become overwhelming and hurt my mental and even physical wellbeing. Over time, it would get even worse and hurt me even more.

I would personally feel very stressed if I was always expected to remain busy. It would honestly make me feel tired and drained to have to maintain the appearance of being busy constantly. This social expectation would be extremely unhealthy and not good for mental health. Overall I can say that my emotions during this expectation would be miserable.

I would probably feel mostly stressed and upset. Being busy can and will take a toll on me as an individual as well as my family, community, or other relationships. I might feel uncomfortable going against the grain or taking more time off when needed. At the same time, being busy is unavoidable so I'd feel good about myself if I learned how to be productive and efficient.

I Would probably feel really stressed. I need a lot of time to myself to relax in order to feel okay, so this would not be okay with me. Societal expectations in of themselves are extremely hard to deal with, and I'm not sure if I could handle being expected to be busy.

I would probably first feel guilty if I felt I wasn't meeting the expectation to be busy. After some time, though, I would feel a little resentful of how that expectation was negatively impacting my emotional state. This might transition into a greater sense of frustration that society's expectations were influencing my life too much.
If I could stay busy consistently then I guess I'd be happy, but the more likely thing is that it'd stress me the fuck out and I would barely be able to get anything done.
If I lived up to this expectation, I would be overwhelmed. I need calm and slow. I cannot always be busy. I will become easily burned out.

In expectation of being busy, I would feel positive and negative feelings. First, positive I would feel motivated. Being that I'm will try hard to be busy and get work done. On the other hand, I will feel negative in stress and worry. Being that I fear that if I don't appear to busy, I might face consequences.

In response to this societal expectation, I often feel bad about myself. I have a disability and can't work full time, so I physically cannot keep up with this societal expectation. It's taken quite a bit of work for me to realize that I'm still a valid person and contributor to my society even if I have to contribute in different ways than most people.

In the face of social expectation to be busy I feel highly stressed. I feel better when I'm not busy but I want to be busy and I want to like to be busy, it's just hard. I've been busy all the time before in college and I was happy for a while but I ended up in the hospital.

In the face of social expectations to be busy I would feel stressed but motivated. I prefer to be busy rather than having nothing to do. I would be determined to do all the work I had to do and complete it so I can relax. I would also feel accomplished after the fact.

In the face of social expectations to be busy, I would feel determined yet overwhelmed. I would want to be accomplished and would feel valued, but would also be thinking about if I was managing/balancing my time well. I would be in constant conflict over if I was keeping busy for myself or for the approval of others. However, I would also maximize my time to be busy with things that interest me, which would add to my overall well-being.

In the face of this social expectation to be busy the main emotion I would feel is stress. I would feel as though I would constantly need to be busy, leading to increased stress levels. I would also feel as though I am inadequate. If I was not busy enough, this would lead to feelings of inadequacy. I would also feel as though my time is listed if I was always busy.
In the face of this social expectation to be busy, I would feel happy. In addition, I would feel excited.

In the social expectation of being busy, I feel like I don't do enough in the day. It makes me feel like I am underperforming in life compared to others. Sometimes I actively try to push more tasks into my day which leads to stress and exhaustion. On days where I feel burned out, I still feel that expectation. Especially at work. On these days I pretend to be busy as to not be viewed negatively by my peers.

In the times that we live in, work and our livelihoods seems to blend with each other.  This can often cause more stress and tiredness.  That is why it is important to allocate time for both.  For instance, I would only allow myself to feel stressful in the work environment.  However, when I go home, I would want to feel relaxed.

In this society, I would be proud to have a lot of responsibility given to me. I would also feel anxious about how busy I might be. I would likely need or want to have some kind of separation between work and home life.

It's exhausting. Constantly. You are expected to be busy, productive, all the time! When you are not productive, you feel guilty. When you are busy working, you're exhausted and wishing for a break, just to stop and be a person for a little while instead of this constant drive to "make this break" or "hustle" and that never seems to end.

It is interesting because I have never thought about this before. Honestly the emotion I am feeling right now is deceit. I say that because I feel like I am always stressed out trying to be the best and accomplish everything that is expected of me, not realizing I was in this catch-22 essentially.

It majorly stresses me out and frustrates me when I feel that I need to appear busy. If I have completed all of my tasks I should not have to be continuously doing something in order to not get complained at. I had a manager that would constantly say negative things to me about appearing busy when I had already done everything that needed doing plus more. It makes life more hectic when you have to be trying to look busy instead of focusing on the things that you are supposed to do. At one point I even broke down and cried because I was pregnant and had done everything that I could. Another employee offered to help me because they knew I could not reach the bottom shelves and I literally got yelled at and told I would be fired if I didn't at least look like I was busy.
It would feel hectic and eventually I probably would be overwhelmed.I would do the best I can but wouldn't want to many days like that.

It would make me anxious and nervous if the social expectation is to be busy. I would feel lazy if I took time to rest. I would feel overwhelmed by trying to find something to do all of the time. I would feel guilty if I ever took time to relax or do something for myself. I would feel like someone would be judging me if I wasn't busy. I would feel negative about life in general. I would feel tired from feeling like I needed to be doing something all the time. I would not enjoy living a life where I was expected to be constantly busy. Relaxation is important to your health and well-being.

It would make me feel good but I think it would make me feel stressed and or irritated as well. I may feel good during the time of being busy, but at some point after I'm going to feel like the day was lost, I got no rest, no relaxtion and no real sense of peace. I just worked worked worked all day long even if it was fun, even if I enjoyed staying busy to look better for others, I am going to regret it later on.

It would make me feel stressed. I only like to be busy when I make myself busy, I don't think it should be expected of me (or anyone) to fill their schedule to the point of always being busy. People's mental health is deprived when they don't have time to relax and appreciate the fruits of their labor.

It would make me stressed to try to appear busy all of the time, and I might try to lengthen tasks so it would look like I was always doing something. I might also feel accomplished if I managed to stay busy or find new tasks to do as I completed old ones. Overall, I think it would increase my stress to live in a society where this is valued, but I could make it work and fit it into my life.

One emotion I feel is anger that people are putting their own expectations on me. Another emotion I feel, though, is satisfaction at actually being busy. I'm conflicted because I want to feel my emotions on my own terms instead of being pressured to feel a certain way. I think that I also feel anxiety both from being expected to feel a certain way as well as being afraid of what people think of me.

Personally, I need time to reflect on my work, so to be in a constant cycle of busyness is not healthy for my psyche. I feel overwhelmed, highly stressed, and depressed if I am ever in that situation. I even notice that I become more irritable, if I am staying busy all the time, which is very out of character for me. Unfortunately, I live in a society, which highly values being busy, but I feel like such a way of living is not healthy for the human soul.
Pressure, anxiety, adrenaline, clammy

Sometimes I feel really overwhelmed and nervous. Some people think being busy is a sign of prestige. You could have more responsibilities and you are thought of as a hard worker. If others see you as not busy or don't think you're working enough they look at you as if you are lazy. I don't like being looked down upon. There's no point in being bus just to say you are doing things if it makes you stressed as it does for me.

The emotions I'd feel in this social expectation to be busy would mainly be stress and motivation. I'd feel stressed that I wouldn't live up to the expectations, but at the same time that would drive me to live up to it, which is why I'd feel motivation as well. The stress would be extreme, for me, because my biggest worry is letting people down, and I'd be worried that I'd be letting people down by not being busy enough. I'd also stress that if I didn't get much done, people would think I was lazy. So there would be many things about it that would stress me out, but also many things about it that would motivate me.

The emotions that I would feel in the social expectation to be busy is that I would feel that I am accomplishing a lot, when I am busy I have the opportunity to work on a lot of the tasks that need to be done and complete them. But at the same time it could also make me feel stress if I am not completing as much as I could or as much as I wanted. Not completing my goals for the day does make me worried and stressed out sometimes.

The emotions that I would feel would be rushed, overwhelmed and exhausted. Keeping up with the work culture takes a tiring toil on my body because we are now more busier than ever compared to  people decades ago because there is more to be accomplished.

The pressure from feeling like I have to be busy would be stressful . I would probably feel anxiety during moments where I am not doing anything, when I have free time, because I would feel like I should be doing something productive with my time. I would feel like I have to tell others that my day is filled with doing stuff.

The social expectation of being busy overwhelms me a great deal. I personally would rather be well rested and have a few things I do wonderfully then being stressed out and tired with a plethora of things I do "ok."

The social expectation to always be busy is stressful. It causes me to be very self-conscious, always asking myself if I am busy enough or if I am busy doing the right things or worry that I will be fired for not being busy. It is frustrating because I feel like I never get a break. Even when I am on vacation people expect to be able to call me or email me and expect me to be working. I try to set clear boundaries, but people tell me that if I do so I will have no future in my career. I don't really believe that's true, but what can I do? I also feel bad turning away work when I am too busy, only to find out that others at my office were even busier than I am and didn't complain. It makes me feel ashamed and lazy, even though my family is telling me that I work too much and they need me. So I feel like I am getting pulled in all directions at the same time and am not doing a good enough job for anyone.

The social expectation to always be doing something and be busy working or moving forward is very stressful. It makes me feel tense just thinking about it. it means you can never take time to rest and just do nothing, enjoying the quiet moments to recharge. Social media makes this worse because people only ever post when they do something they want others to know that they are doing.

The social expectation to be busy is personally exhausting and unproductive. It is unreasonable to just go and go all the time without any room for rest. It is how we quickly become unmotivated and uninspired. It is also not very fulfilling. In my opinion, the social expectation to be busy really speaks to the overall impact of capitalism. Regardless of the social expectation, rest and relaxation are necessary.

There is definitely a stigma in society that not being busy is synonymous their laziness, which is undersirable. I feel this at work sometimes, on the occasions when I am out of work or am not that busy, I will sometimes feel guilty like I am not working to my full potential. Same with my free time, it sometimes feels like if you have a weekend day with nothing to do you are wasting your time. I think this is unfair, as sometimes doing nothing can be good for your mental health. Personally, I think I am okay with not being busy, and handle these scenarios by taking them as a time to relax, rather than feeling bad about myself.
This makes me feel just a little bit anxious. I worry that I will not be able to meet this expectation at all times. I feel like the effort may just be too much to handle on certain days.

This would feel stressful to me. The constant need to always be busy would be overbearing. No time to just relax and and enjoy yourself would lead to such a toxic makeup of emotion. I need my own time to decompress and relax and if I were pressured to never stop moving or working I would ultimately crumble under the pressure.
Tired, really tired. Also, somewhat proud for naturally meeting these expectations. A little confused as to why people view others more as robots rather than people. Also, a little amused.

Under the social pressure to remain busy, I would feel frustrated and resentful. I feel as though I am the only person who can determine what qualifies and successful and unsuccessful. Our society often attributed business to success as if they’re synonymous words, but I wholeheartedly disagree with that mentality. Regardless of how I feel, there are still consequences if I am perceived as underperforming by my peers. That’s when the frustration and discouragement would settle in.
What I feel on the aspect of needing to be busy is that I feel kind of okay with it. Being busy isn't that bad if you striving to go after your goals.

When I know I am expected to be busy, like in a retail workspace where if you're not actively doing something you are considered to be stealing company time, I feel a little stressed and a lot of pressure to always be outwardly, obviously doing something. I will sometimes even put off doing things that aren't as obvious because if they get done but the big, flashy things don't get done, there's not as much proof that something got done and I feel like I didn't live up to people's expectations of my work ethic, even though I know that's not true and that all work needs to get done and is therefore important.

Whenever people have an expectation that you should always feel busy I get annoyed. I feel like if you're busy to the point where you need to tell people about it, then you're not being efficient with your time. I like being able to fill my day with activities, but if I'm stressed about it, then what's the point? Other people seem to brag about being "so busy" like it's a badge of honor, but to me they just sound like they don't know how to manage their time well. Being busy to me has negative connotations, whereas having a full day is a much more desirable goal. Busyness suggests disorder and stress, but a structured, well-planned day full of tasks is a good use of one's time.

While I myself also like to be busy at most times, it is unfair of our society to expect people to ALWAYS be busy. Not only can being constantly busy become very stressful and overwhelming, it can also cause a great many health issues, such as over tiredness, over worked heart, and even depression. Often times people feel as though they are being “lazy” if they are not constantly in the midst of something. Human beings need rest, relaxation, and leisure activities just as much as any other animal. For example, when a monkey is reclining in a tree, maybe eating a banana or playing with a twig, we do not judge it as being “lazy” or without motivation. Why shouldn’t the same be true for our fellow humans?
